Here are two versions of the email. The first is written in a professional, academic tone suitable for a professor. The second is written in an informal, casual tone suitable for a relaxed audience or a peer.

### Option 1: Professional & Academic (For a Professor)

**Subject:** Extension Request: [Course Name] Project – [Your Name]

Dear Professor [Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name], which is currently due on [Original Due Date]. Due to [briefly state reason, e.g., unforeseen personal circumstances / a need to ensure the quality of my research], I am unable to finalize the work by the original deadline.

I am committed to submitting high-quality work and would appreciate the opportunity to extend the deadline by [Number] days. I can guarantee that the completed project will be submitted by [New Proposed Date].

Thank you for your time and consideration.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Student ID Number]

***

### Option 2: Informal & Casual (For a Casual Audience)

**Subject:** Quick question about the project deadline

Hey [Name],

Hope you're having a good week!

I'm reaching out because I'm running into a bit of a snag with the [Project Name] and I'm worried about rushing it. Would it be possible to get a short extension? I could have it done by [New Date] instead of [Original Date].

Let me know if that works for you. Thanks a lot!

Best,

[Your Name]
